rationale: The cited paper is used as a central prior benchmark for the same methodological comparison in this article. In the literature review, the authors explicitly position their study against Ding, Grewal, and Liechty (2005) as the prior first attempt to compare OE, BDM, CBC, and ICBC with real purchases, and they state how their own analysis differs. They also use this citation again in the results and conclusion to interpret replicated patterns (for example, more none choices and higher price sensitivity under incentive alignment), which shows an active framing and interpretation role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper directly builds on Ding, Grewal, and Liechty (2005)'s four-method comparison framework and extends it by testing additional evaluation criteria, including mean WTP, demand-curve fit, and pricing decision outcomes against a real-purchase benchmark.
